title: T7: Hook threshold configuration externalization
description: Q-11 resolved: hook timing thresholds must be configurable. Move 3 hardcoded constants in `fabric-hint.cjs` to `.fabric/fabric-config.json`: `THRESHOLD_HOURS` (24) → `archive_hint_hours`, `THRESHOLD_PENDING_COUNT` (10) → `review_hint_pending_count`, `THRESHOLD_PENDING_AGE_DAYS` (7) → `review_hint_pending_age_days`. Add new `maintenance_hint_days` (default 14) + `maintenance_hint_cooldown_days` (default 7) — these are prerequisites for T10's Signal D. Create new `docs/configuration.md` enumerating all config fields with small/medium/large repo recommendations.

files.change: (if exists as init template) mirror the new fields with defaults so `fabric init` produces a config with all hook tunables
create new helper: `loadHookConfig()` returns merged-with-defaults config object; cached per-process; handles missing file by returning defaults
replace 3 hardcoded constants with `config.archive_hint_hours`/`config.review_hint_pending_count`/`config.review_hint_pending_age_days`; export the new maintenance_* fields for T10 to consume
extend Zod schema for fabric-config.json with the 5 new fields (each with default value)

create new doc: section per config field with default + small (<20 entries) / medium (20-100) / large (>100) repo recommendations; include T11 --apply-lint safety section pointing to FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE env var
test: hook reads config via loadHookConfig; missing config file → defaults applied; partial config → merged with defaults
verify all 5 new config fields are readable from fabric-config.json and consumed by fabric-hint.cjs

implementation: Define Zod schema with .default() on each new field so missing fields use defaults
Implement config-loader.cjs: synchronous fs.readFileSync (this is a hook, must be fast); JSON.parse → Zod parse → cache in module-level variable; handle ENOENT by returning Zod-derived defaults
Refactor fabric-hint.cjs: replace each constant reference with `config.fieldName`; verify Signal A/B/C banners still trigger at the same defaults
Update .fabric/fabric-config.json with the new fields (preserving any existing fields)
Write docs/configuration.md with table + per-field section + repo-size recommendation matrix
Update init template (if applicable) so new repos get all fields by default
Coverage gate: programmatic check that fabric-hint.cjs branches on config-derived values

reference.pattern: Existing fabric-config.json structure (rc.5 schema); .fabric/ directory layout
reference.examples: Config loader pattern: load once per process, fail open (use defaults) rather than crash

criteria: GIVEN .fabric/fabric-config.json has `archive_hint_hours: 48` WHEN fabric-hint.cjs runs THEN Signal A only fires after 48h elapsed (not the hardcoded 24h)
GIVEN .fabric/fabric-config.json is missing entirely WHEN fabric-hint.cjs runs THEN loadHookConfig returns all 5 fields with documented defaults (24, 10, 7, 14, 7) and no crash
GIVEN partial config (only `archive_hint_hours: 48` set) WHEN config loaded THEN all other fields fall back to their defaults
docs/configuration.md exists and documents all 5 new fields + small/medium/large repo recommendation table
scripts/rc7-coverage-gate.mjs assertion: all 5 fields readable from fabric-config.json + consumed by fabric-hint.cjs branches

rationale.chosen_approach: Single config file + Zod schema with defaults + synchronous loader — config is small, hooks need synchronous load, defaults baked into schema means no parallel constants
decision_factors: Q-11 explicit user need for tunability
Defaults-in-schema avoids drift between schema and code
rationale.tradeoffs: Schema becomes source of truth — if defaults need to change, must update schema not just code; accepted (correctness)

manual_checks: Edit .fabric/fabric-config.json to set archive_hint_hours=1 → trigger Stop hook after 70 minutes → verify Signal A fires
Delete .fabric/fabric-config.json → trigger hook → verify defaults applied without error
success_metrics: Hook load time <50ms even with config parse
Config field count: 5 new fields visible in docs/configuration.md table
unit: loadHookConfig returns defaults when file missing
Zod schema validates partial config + merges defaults
fabric-hint thresholds derive from config-loader output
integration: End-to-end: edit config → run hook → verify behavior matches new threshold
